### package.json with bun-create setup logic

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/runtime/templating/create.mdx

Example `package.json` demonstrating `preinstall`, `postinstall`, and `start` scripts within the `bun-create` section for local templates.

```json
{
  "name": "@bun-examples/simplereact",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "main": "index.js",
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "^17.0.2",
    "react-dom": "^17.0.2"
  },
  "bun-create": {
    "preinstall": "echo 'Installing...'",
    "postinstall": ["echo 'Done!'"],
    "start": "bun run echo 'Hello world!'"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Run the start script

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Command to execute the 'start' script defined in package.json using Bun.

```bash
bun run start
```

--------------------------------

### Manual CI Setup - Install packages

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Commands to manually install required packages and then run the tests.

```bash
apt-get update && apt-get install -y libsecret-1-dev gnome-keyring dbus-x11

bun test test/js/bun/secrets.test.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies and run development server

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/solidstart.mdx

After initializing the project, navigate into the new directory (`my-app`), install the project dependencies using `bun install`, and then start the development server with `bun dev`. The server will typically run on `http://localhost:3000/`.

```sh
cd my-app
bun install
```

```sh
bun dev
```

```txt
$ vinxi dev
vinxi v0.5.8
vinxi starting dev server

  ➜ Local:    http://localhost:3000/
  ➜ Network:  use --host to expose
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Usage

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/cli/install.mdx

Examples of installing packages, specific versions, and tags.

```bash
bun install react
bun install react@19.1.1 # specific version
bun install react@latest # specific tag
```

--------------------------------

### Add start script to package.json

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

This JSON snippet shows how to add a 'start' script to your package.json file, which executes 'bun run index.ts'.

```json
{
  "name": "quickstart",
  "module": "index.ts",
  "type": "module",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "start": "bun run index.ts"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/bun": "latest"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Example package.json with non-npm dependencies

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/cli/install.mdx

Demonstrates installing dependencies from Git, GitHub, and local/remote tarballs.

```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "dayjs": "git+https://github.com/iamkun/dayjs.git",
    "lodash": "git+ssh://github.com/lodash/lodash.git#4.17.21",
    "moment": "git@github.com:moment/moment.git",
    "zod": "github:colinhacks/zod",
    "react": "https://registry.npmjs.org/react/-/react-18.2.0.tgz",
    "bun-types": "npm:@types/bun"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Run the HTTP server

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Runs the updated `index.ts` file to start the HTTP server.

```bash
bun run index.ts
```

```txt
Listening on http://localhost:3000
```

--------------------------------

### Setup commands

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/packages/bun-lambda/README.md

Commands to clone the repository, set up sparse checkout, install dependencies, and publish the layer.

```sh
git clone --filter=blob:none --sparse https://github.com/oven-sh/bun.git
git -C bun sparse-checkout set packages/bun-lambda
cd bun/packages/bun-lambda
bun install
bun run publish-layer
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions (Explicit Package Installation)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Example of running Bun secrets tests in GitHub Actions with explicit installation of keyring packages.

```yaml
- name: Install keyring packages  
  run: |
    sudo apt-get update
    sudo apt-get install -y libsecret-1-dev gnome-keyring dbus-x11

- name: Run secrets tests
  run: bun test test/js/bun/secrets.test.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Setup

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/runtime/http/server.mdx

Example demonstrating how to set up a basic HTTP server with Bun.serve, including static, dynamic, and method-specific routes, as well as file serving and redirects.

```ts
const server = Bun.serve({
  // `routes` requires Bun v1.2.3+
  routes: {
    // Static routes
    "/api/status": new Response("OK"),

    // Dynamic routes
    "/users/:id": req => {
      return new Response(`Hello User ${req.params.id}!`);
    },

    // Per-HTTP method handlers
    "/api/posts": {
      GET: () => new Response("List posts"),
      POST: async req => {
        const body = await req.json();
        return Response.json({ created: true, ...body });
      },
    },

    // Wildcard route for all routes that start with "/api/" and aren't otherwise matched
    "/api/*": Response.json({ message: "Not found" }, { status: 404 }),

    // Redirect from /blog/hello to /blog/hello/world
    "/blog/hello": Response.redirect("/blog/hello/world"),

    // Serve a file by lazily loading it into memory
    "/favicon.ico": Bun.file("./favicon.ico"),
  },

  // (optional) fallback for unmatched routes:
  // Required if Bun's version < 1.2.3
  fetch(req) {
    return new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 });
  },
});

console.log(`Server running at ${server.url}`);
```

--------------------------------

### Required Packages Installation

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Command to install necessary packages on Ubuntu/Debian systems.

```bash
apt-get install -y \
  libsecret-1-dev \
  gnome-keyring \
  dbus-x11
```

--------------------------------

### Putting it all together

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/packages/bun-uws/misc/READMORE.md

A complete example demonstrating route definition, listening on a port, and running the event loop with `App.run`.

```c++
int main() {
    uWS::App().get("/*", [](auto *res, auto *req) {
        res->end("Hello World!");
    }).listen(9001, [](auto *listenSocket) {
        if (listenSocket) {
            std::cout << "Listening for connections..." << std::endl;
        }
    }).run();

    std::cout << "Shoot! We failed to listen and the App fell through, exiting now!" << std::endl;
}
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions (Auto-Setup)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Example of running Bun secrets tests in GitHub Actions with automatic setup.

```yaml
- name: Run secrets tests (auto-setup)
  run: bun test test/js/bun/secrets.test.ts
```

--------------------------------

### React Project Template Examples

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/snippets/cli/init.mdx

Examples of initializing React projects with different configurations using the `--react` option, shown as a single command line.

```bash
bun init --react bun init --react=tailwind bun init --react=shadcn
```

--------------------------------

### BuildKite

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Example of running Bun secrets tests in BuildKite.

```yaml
steps:
  - command: bun test test/js/bun/secrets.test.ts
    label: "🔐 Secrets API Tests"
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new project

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Initializes a new Bun project named 'my-app'.

```bash
bun init my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Define an HTTP route and start a server

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/elysia.mdx

Example of defining a simple GET route and starting an Elysia server on port 8080.

```ts
import { Elysia } from "elysia";

const app = new Elysia().get("/", () => "Hello Elysia").listen(8080);

console.log(`🦊 Elysia is running at on port ${app.server?.port}...`);
```

--------------------------------

### Docker

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Example of a Dockerfile for running Bun secrets tests, including optional pre-installation of packages.

```dockerfile
# Optional: pre-install packages for faster test startup
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    libsecret-1-dev \
    gnome-keyring \
    dbus-x11

# Run test normally - setup is automatic
RUN bun test test/js/bun/secrets.test.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Run the initial Bun app

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Navigates into the project directory and runs the default `index.ts` file.

```bash
cd my-app
bun run index.ts
```

```txt
Hello via Bun!
```

--------------------------------

### React Project Initialization Example

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/snippets/cli/init.mdx

Example of initializing a basic React project.

```bash
bun init --react
```

--------------------------------

### Set credential with unrestricted access for CI

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Example of using allowUnrestrictedAccess: true for Bun.secrets.set in CI environments on macOS.

```ts
// For CI environments where user interaction is not possible
await Bun.secrets.set({
  service: "ci-deployment", 
  name: "api-key",
  value: process.env.API_KEY,
  allowUnrestrictedAccess: true // Bypass macOS keychain user prompts
});
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Bun setup in GitHub Actions

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/runtime/cicd.mdx

This snippet shows how to use the `setup-bun` action and run basic `bun` commands like `bun install`, `bun index.ts`, and `bun run build`.

```yaml
name: my-workflow
jobs:
  my-job:
    name: my-job
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # ...
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      # run any `bun` or `bunx` command
      - run: bun install
      - run: bun index.ts
      - run: bun run build
```

--------------------------------

### Delete credential with empty string

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Examples showing how to delete a credential using Bun.secrets.delete or by setting an empty string with Bun.secrets.set.

```ts
// These are equivalent:
await Bun.secrets.delete({ service: "myapp", name: "token" });
await Bun.secrets.set({ service: "myapp", name: "token", value: "" });
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new project with Blank template

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Initializes a new Bun project named 'my-app' and selects the 'Blank' template, showing the created files.

```bash
bun init my-app
```

```txt
✓ Select a project template: Blank

- .gitignore
- CLAUDE.md
- .cursor/rules/use-bun-instead-of-node-vite-npm-pnpm.mdc -> CLAUDE.md
- index.ts
- tsconfig.json (for editor autocomplete)
- README.md
```

--------------------------------

### Run Downloaded Release Build

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Example of running a specific release build downloaded via `bun-pr`.

```sh
bun-1234566 --version
```

--------------------------------

### Install Settings Inheritance Example

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/test/configuration.mdx

Example `bunfig.toml` demonstrating how `[install]` settings are inherited by `bun test`.

```toml
[install]
# These settings are inherited by bun test
registry = "https://npm.company.com/"
exact = true
prefer = "offline"

[test]
# Test-specific configuration
coverage = true
timeout = 10000

```

--------------------------------

### Create index.html

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Creates a basic HTML file to be served by the Bun server.

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Bun</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <h1>Bun!</h1>
  </body>
</html>
```

--------------------------------

### Use a globally installed package

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/cli/install.mdx

Example of executing a command-line tool installed globally.

```bash
cowsay "Bun!"
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Example: Build Command (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/bundler/index.mdx

Building the basic example using Bun's CLI.

```bash
bun build ./index.tsx --outdir ./out
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Workflow with bun install

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/cli/install.mdx

Example GitHub Actions workflow to install Bun and project dependencies using `bun install`.

```yaml
name: bun-types
jobs:
  build:
    name: build-app
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install
      - name: Build app
        run: bun run build
```

--------------------------------

### Accept All Defaults Example

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/snippets/cli/init.mdx

Example of initializing a project by accepting all default prompts.

```bash
bun init -y
```

--------------------------------

### Force Keyring Setup

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Command to force the automatic keyring setup using an environment variable.

```bash
FORCE_KEYRING_SETUP=1 bun test test/js/bun/secrets.test.ts
```

### Navigate and install dependencies

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/vite.mdx

Change into the project directory and install dependencies using Bun.

```bash
cd my-app
bun install
```

### cowsay output example

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/cli/add.mdx

Example output from the globally installed `cowsay` command.

```txt
 ______
< Bun! >
 ------
        \   ^__^
         \  (oo)\_______
            (__)\       )\/\
                ||----w |
                ||     ||
```

### Automatic CI Setup

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/secrets-ci-setup.md

Command to run the secrets test, which automatically handles setup in CI environments.

```bash
bun test test/js/bun/secrets.test.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Configure global installation behavior

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/cli/add.mdx

Example `bunfig.toml` configuration for global installation paths.

```toml
[install]
# where `bun add --global` installs packages
globalDir = "~/.bun/install/global"

# where globally-installed package bins are linked
globalBinDir = "~/.bun/bin"
```

--------------------------------

### Install figlet package and types

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Installs the `figlet` package and its TypeScript type declarations.

```bash
bun add figlet
bun add -d @types/figlet # TypeScript users only
```

--------------------------------

### Start the dev server

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/tanstack-start.mdx

Change to the project directory and run the dev server with Bun.

```sh
cd my-tanstack-app
bun --bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies for filtered packages

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/filter.mdx

Examples of using `bun install` with `--filter` to install dependencies for specific packages in a monorepo.

```bash
# Install dependencies for all workspaces except `pkg-c`
bun install --filter '!pkg-c'

# Install dependencies for packages in `./packages` (`pkg-a`, `pkg-b`, `pkg-c`)
bun install --filter './packages/*'

# Save as above, but exclude the root package.json
bun install --filter '!./' --filter './packages/*'
```

--------------------------------

### pnpm-workspace.yaml Example

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/cli/install.mdx

Example of a pnpm workspace configuration file, showing packages and catalog definitions.

```yaml
packages:
  - "apps/*"
  - "packages/*"

catalog:
  react: ^18.0.0
  typescript: ^5.0.0

catalogs:
  build:
    webpack: ^5.0.0
    babel: ^7.0.0
```

--------------------------------

### Create a minimal HTTP server

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Replaces `index.ts` content with code to create an HTTP server listening on port 3000.

```ts
const server = Bun.serve({
  port: 3000,
  routes: {
    "/": () => new Response('Bun!')
  }
});

console.log(`Listening on ${server.url}`);
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Bun.build() Executable Compilation Example

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/bundler/executables.mdx

Demonstrates a full `Bun.build()` configuration for creating an executable, including a custom plugin, minification, sourcemaps, and environment definitions.

```ts
import type { BunPlugin } from "bun";

const myPlugin: BunPlugin = {
  name: "my-plugin",
  setup(build) {
    // Plugin implementation
  },
};

const result = await Bun.build({
  entrypoints: ["./src/cli.ts"],
  compile: {
    target: "bun-linux-x64",
    outfile: "./dist/mycli",
    execArgv: ["--smol"],
    autoloadDotenv: false,
    autoloadBunfig: false,
  },
  minify: true,
  sourcemap: "linked",
  bytecode: true,
  define: {
    "process.env.NODE_ENV": JSON.stringify("production"),
    VERSION: JSON.stringify("1.0.0"),
  },
  plugins: [myPlugin],
});

if (result.success) {
  console.log("Build successful:", result.outputs[0].path);
}
```

--------------------------------

### Use Setup and Teardown

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/test/writing-tests.mdx

Example demonstrating the use of `beforeEach` and `afterEach` for test setup and teardown.

```ts
import { beforeEach, afterEach, test } from "bun:test";

let testUser;

beforeEach(() => {
  testUser = createTestUser();
});

afterEach(() => {
  cleanupTestUser(testUser);
});

test("should update user profile", () => {
  // Use testUser in test
});
```

--------------------------------

### Start development server

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/sveltekit.mdx

Navigate into the project directory and start the SvelteKit development server using Bun.

```sh
cd my-app
bun --bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Defining workspaces in package.json

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/install/from-npm-install-to-bun-install.mdx

Example of how to define workspaces in a package.json file.

```json
{
  "name": "my-app",
  "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Serve HTML file from root

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Modifies `index.ts` to import `index.html` and serve it from the root `/` route.

```ts
import figlet from 'figlet';
import index from './index.html'; // [!code ++]

const server = Bun.serve({
  port: 3000,
  routes: {
    "/": index, // [!code ++]
    "/figlet": () => {
      const body = figlet.textSync('Bun!');
      return new Response(body);
    }
  }
});

console.log(`Listening on ${server.url}`);
```

--------------------------------

### Advanced Bun Build and Execution Examples

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/CLAUDE.md

Examples demonstrating various build configurations, including debug, release, and local WebKit builds, with direct execution of tests or scripts.

```sh
bun bd test foo.test.ts                    # debug build + quiet debug logs
```

```sh
bun run build test foo.test.ts             # debug build
```

```sh
bun run build:release -p 'Bun.version'     # release build
```

```sh
bun run build:local run script.ts          # debug build with local WebKit
```

--------------------------------

### Install Bun TypeScript types

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/quickstart.mdx

Installs `@types/bun` as a development dependency to resolve TypeScript errors.

```bash
bun add -d @types/bun
```

--------------------------------

### Build and run the application

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/tanstack-start.mdx

Commands to build the application and start the server.

```sh
bun run build
bun run start
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Example: Build Command (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/bundler/index.mdx

Building the basic example using Bun's JavaScript API.

```typescript
await Bun.build({
  entrypoints: ["./index.tsx"],
  outdir: "./out",
});
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies for specific packages using --filter

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/pm/cli/install.mdx

Examples of using the `--filter` flag to install dependencies for a subset of packages in a monorepo.

```bash
# Install dependencies for all workspaces except `pkg-c`
bun install --filter '!pkg-c'

# Install dependencies for only `pkg-a` in `./packages/pkg-a`
bun install --filter './packages/pkg-a'
```

--------------------------------

### Start a server (`Bun.listen()`)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/runtime/networking/tcp.mdx

Basic example of starting a TCP server with Bun.listen, defining socket event handlers.

```typescript
Bun.listen({
  hostname: "localhost",
  port: 8080,
  socket: {
    data(socket, data) {}, // message received from client
    open(socket) {}, // socket opened
    close(socket, error) {}, // socket closed
    drain(socket) {}, // socket ready for more data
    error(socket, error) {}, // error handler
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Bun Project

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/gel.mdx

Create a new directory and initialize a Bun project.

```sh
mkdir my-edgedb-app
cd my-edgedb-app
bun init -y
```

--------------------------------

### Ordered list start number beginning with zero

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/md/spec.txt

Example of an ordered list with a start number beginning with 0.

```markdown
0. ok
```

### Start the dev server

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/nextjs.mdx

Change to the project directory and run the dev server with Bun.

```sh
cd my-bun-app
bun --bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Ordered list start number with leading zeros

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/md/spec.txt

Example of an ordered list with a start number containing leading zeros.

```markdown
003. ok
```

--------------------------------

### Quickstart

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/runtime/shell.mdx

Demonstrates using Bun Shell to pipe a fetch response as stdin to `cat` and then `wc -c`.

```ts
import { $ } from "bun";

const response = await fetch("https://example.com");

// Use Response as stdin.
await $`cat < ${response} | wc -c`; // 1256
```

--------------------------------

### Valid ordered list start number

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/md/spec.txt

Example of an ordered list with a valid start number (nine digits or less).

```markdown
123456789. ok
```

--------------------------------

### Run in Deno (`Deno.serve`)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/bench/websocket-server/README.md

Command to start the websocket server using Deno.

```bash
deno run -A ./chat-server.deno.mjs
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a SolidStart app

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/solidstart.mdx

Initialize a SolidStart app using `bun create solid` with the `--solidstart` and `--ts` flags. When prompted, select the `basic` template for a minimal starter app. The output shows the project creation details and next steps.

```sh
bun create solid my-app --solidstart --ts
```

```txt
┌
 Create-Solid v0.6.11
│
◇  Project Name
│  my-app
│
◇  Which template would you like to use?
│  basic
│
◇  Project created 🎉
│
◇  To get started, run: ─╮
│                        │
│  cd my-app             │
│  bun install           │
│  bun dev               │
│                        │
├────────────────────────╯
```

--------------------------------

### Install a specific version (Windows)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/util/upgrade.mdx

Use the install script to get a particular Bun version on Windows.

```powershell
iex "& {$(irm https://bun.sh/install.ps1)} -Version 1.3.3"
```

--------------------------------

### Invalid ordered list start number (negative)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/test/js/bun/md/spec.txt

Example of an ordered list with an invalid negative start number, which is not parsed as a list.

```markdown
-1. not ok
```

--------------------------------

### Install Bun (Native)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Install a release build of Bun using the native installer script.

```bash
$ curl -fsSL https://bun.com/install | bash
```

--------------------------------

### Defining a GET route

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/packages/bun-uws/misc/READMORE.md

Example of attaching behavior to a URL route using the `get` method with `uWS::App`.

```c++
uWS::App().get("/hello", [](auto *res, auto *req) {
    res->end("Hello World!");
});
```

--------------------------------

### Start development server with bunx

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/vite.mdx

Run the Vite development server using `bunx` with the `--bun` flag.

```bash
bunx --bun vite
```

--------------------------------

### Install ccache (openSUSE)

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Install ccache on openSUSE using zypper.

```bash
# For openSUSE
$ sudo zypper install ccache
```

--------------------------------

### Redis String Operations

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/runtime/redis.mdx

Examples of setting, getting, deleting, checking existence, setting expiration, and getting time-to-live for Redis string keys.

```ts
// Set a key
await redis.set("user:1:name", "Alice");

// Get a key
const name = await redis.get("user:1:name");

// Get a key as Uint8Array
const buffer = await redis.getBuffer("user:1:name");

// Delete a key
await redis.del("user:1:name");

// Check if a key exists
const exists = await redis.exists("user:1:name");

// Set expiration (in seconds)
await redis.set("session:123", "active");
await redis.expire("session:123", 3600); // expires in 1 hour

// Get time to live (in seconds)
const ttl = await redis.ttl("session:123");
```

--------------------------------

### test-setup.ts

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/test/configuration.mdx

Example of a global test setup script.

```typescript
// Global test setup
import { beforeAll, afterAll } from "bun:test";

beforeAll(() => {
  // Set up test database
  setupTestDatabase();
});

afterAll(() => {
  // Clean up
  cleanupTestDatabase();
});
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Vite project

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/ecosystem/vite.mdx

Initialize a new Vite project using Bun's `create` command.

```bash
bun create vite my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Running scripts and executables with Bun

Source: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/blob/main/docs/guides/install/from-npm-install-to-bun-install.mdx

Examples of how to run package.json scripts, executables, and JavaScript/TypeScript files using `bun`.

```sh
# Run a package.json script:
bun my-script
bun run my-script

# Run an executable in node_modules/.bin:
bun my-executable # such as tsc, esbuild, etc.
bun run my-executable

# Run a JavaScript/TypeScript file:
bun ./index.ts
```
